rulings.Case Title: CHATEAU DE LOUIS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al VS. ERIC AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment Enforcing Liability On Bond
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 14, 2010, line 1. DEFENDANT JOHN KENDALL'S Motion For Judgment Enforcing Liability On Bond is denied without prejudice. The court finds that trustee has standing to bring this motion but has not met its evidentiary burden under C.C.P. section 490.020. The court rejects offset argument based on language of C.C.P. section 490.040. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: MONA FIELD et al VS. DEBRA BOWEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Preliminary Injunction And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 14, 2010, line 5. PLAINTIFFS MONA FIELD, RICHARD WINGER, STEPHEN CHESSIN, JENNIFER WOZNIAK, JEFF MACKLER, RODNEY MARTIN'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is denied. Plaintiffs have standing to file their claims and bring this motion. Storer v. Brown (1974) 415 U.S. 724, 737 n 8. However, plaintiffs fail to show a likelihood of success on the merits. It is constitutional to ban write-in voting under U.S. and California Supreme Court precedent. (See Burdick v. Takushi (1992) 504 U.S. 428; Edelstein v. C.C.S.F. (2002) 29 Cal. 4th 164). When Election Code sections 8141.5 and 8606 are read together, it is apparent that the Legislature intended to ban write-ins in the general election. (See also comments of the Secretary of State and the Assembly Bill Analysis). Insufficient evidence and case law support the argument that the party preference ban violates the Equal Protection Clause or the Elections Clause. The state may require candidates not affiliated with qualified parties to use the "independent" label. (See Libertarian Party v. Eu (1980) 28 Cal 3d 535). Several federal circuit courts have also held that a state is not constitutionally obligated to permit candidates to list their preferred party label on the ballot. (See Schrader v. Blackwell 241 F. 3d 783 (6th Cir. 2001); McLaughlin v. No. Carolina Bd. of Elec. 65 F. 3d 1215 (4th Cir. 1995); Lightfoot v. Eu 964 F 2d. 865 (9th Cir. 1992); Rubin v. City of Santa Monica (308 F 3d 1008 (9th Cir. 2002)). Plaintiffs showing of imminent harm is not sufficient. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: FIRST AMENDMENT COALITION VS. CALIFORNIA PUBLIC EMPLOYEES RETIREMENT SYSTEM
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearipng On Petition For Writ Of Mandate Under The Public Records Act
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 14, 2010, line 6. PETITIONER FIRST AMENDMENT COALITION'S Petition For Writ Of Mandate Under The Public Records Act. Writ of mandate is granted. Court adopts petitioner's proposed order, except order must be modified to reflect that an amicus brief was filed by Page Mill, and parties to meet and confer regarding timing of preparation of privilege log for attorney-client documents and when disclosure of non-attorney-client priveleged documents will take place. Ptitioner's evidentiary objections are overruled. =(302/CWW)
